slides.title: When 'OR' isn't Enough ➕
slides.content:
A Union (`|`) means 'this OR that'.
An **Intersection** (`&`) means 'this AND that'.

Intersections are heavily used to merge multiple object types into one massive object type.

slides.title: The Ampersand &
slides.content: You combine Types together using the ampersand `&`.

quizData.question: What happens if a variable typed as `ColorfulCircle` (above) is missing the `radius` property?

options.text: TypeScript throws an error because the intersection demands ALL properties from ALL intersecting types.
It ignores the `Circle` part of the intersection.

quizData.explanation: Because it's an 'AND' relationship, the object must mathematically satisfy the requirements of every intersected type.

slides.title: The Impossible Intersection 🕳️
slides.content:
Be careful when intersecting primitive types! What happens if you try `type Impossible = string & number;`?

Since a value cannot biologically be a string AND a number simultaneously, TypeScript evaluates it to completely impossible!

slides.title: The 'never' Type 🛑
slides.content: When TS hits an impossible intersection (like `string & number`), it assigns it the `never` type. A variable of type `never` can mathematically never hold any value!
